media.description: There was a reason the Miami Heat let it fly when it came to their lone selection in the NBA draft, including spending $1.5 million to move up four spots for Louisville guard Ryan Conwell.

items.title: [Windhorst] The Miami Heat are expected to lose Norman Powell in free agency unless he takes a huge discount
items.author: oklolzzzzs
items.body_excerpt: “They’re gonna lose their All Star, Norman Powell. Unless he takes a crazy discount. He’s gonna be gone… Andy Ellisburg their GM, is superior at his job. Erik Spoelstra, their developmental staff, I’m betting on the Heat. Just not in 2026-2027”

items.title: Andrew Wiggins declining his player option and re-signing on a long-term deal at a lower starting salary could create enough apron space to re-sign Norman Powell.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Andrew Wiggins declining his player option and re-signing on a long-term deal at a lower starting salary could create enough apron space to re-sign Norman Powell.

But after seeing some of today's trades and contracts, Wiggins' market could be too strong for that to be feasible.

items.title: This Heat roster needs shooting. They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with his Bird rights and even extend him now.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
This Heat roster needs shooting. They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with his Bird rights and even extend him now.

They'll need to reduce their payroll to create space to re-sign him.

They could also use more flexibility to open up the full $15M mid-level.

media.title: ASK IRA: Is it a given that if Tyler Herro goes then Norman Powell returns for Heat?
media.description: Q: I’m trying not to get my hopes up, but it’s seeming more and more likely that we could end up with Giannis Antetokounmpo. It also appears Tyler Herro, Jaime Jaquez Jr. and Kel’el Ware will be gone ...
